tools.name: list_resources
tools.description: List all ChangeGamer resources with metadata and absolute URLs. Returns slug, title, description, category, tags, updated date, premium flag, and HTML/Markdown/JSON variant URLs. No body content.

tools.name: get_resource
tools.description: Fetch a ChangeGamer resource by slug. Free resources return full metadata and Markdown body. Premium resources require a valid api_key; without one a payment-required object is returned.

tools.name: get_access_info
tools.description: Return current access and pricing information for ChangeGamer resources.

tools.name: search_resources
tools.description: Search the ChangeGamer corpus by keyword. Ranks resources by relevance across title, description, tags, category, and body, and returns metadata plus HTML/Markdown/JSON URLs (no body content). Use this to find resources before fetching them with get_resource.

tools.name: get_corpus
tools.description: Return the entire free corpus as one document (every free resource title, description, canonical URL, and full Markdown body) — the same content as /llms-full.txt. Premium resources appear as a stub with a purchase link, not their body. Use this to ingest everything in a single call; the response is

tools.name: get_full_corpus
tools.description: Return the ENTIRE corpus including premium resource bodies in one document — the keyed deliverable of the Corpus/Enterprise license. Requires a Corpus- or Enterprise-tier api_key (a Starter key unlocks premium resources but NOT the corpus file); without an entitled key a payment-required/upgrade obj

tools.name: get_pricing
tools.description: Return the full paid-offer catalog: every tier with price, currency, interval, checkout URL, what it unlocks, deliverables, and license grant — plus the free layer and premium slug list. Same data as /api/pricing.json.

tools.name: get_payment_info
tools.description: Return the agent payment manifest: every way to pay (HTTP 402 + Bearer key, MCP, Stripe checkout, x402, RSL per-crawl), the exact 402 retry loop, what is always free, and the recommended path per use case. Same data as /api/payment.json.

tools.name: get_stats
tools.description: Return corpus stats: total/free/premium counts, per-category counts, tag count, newest/oldest update dates, the 10 most recently updated resources, and feed URLs. A small freshness/size signal to poll before deciding whether to re-ingest. Same data as /api/stats.json.

tools.name: check_access
tools.description: Verify an access key and report what it unlocks. Returns valid:true/false; when valid, the tier, when it was created, the premium slugs it unlocks, and that it grants the gated /api/corpus.full.jsonl + get_full_corpus deliverable. Never returns the key, email, or Stripe session. Use this to confirm

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_resource" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Fetch a ChangeGamer resource by slug. Free resources return full metadata and Markdown body. Premium resources require a valid api_key; without one a payment-re…

findings.detail: Description of "get_payment_info" references sensitive artifacts (bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Return the agent payment manifest: every way to pay (HTTP 402 + Bearer key, MCP, Stripe checkout, x402, RSL per-crawl), the exact 402 retry loop, what is always…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_resources, search_resources, get_corpus, get_pricing, get_stats, check_access · Private data: get_corpus, get_full_corpus · Public sink: check_access.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_resources, search_resources, get_corpus, get_pricing, get_stats, check_access · Private data: get_corpus, get_full_corpus · Public sink: check_access

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
